name: Savory Poached Carp (Hot or Jelled)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Large Lemon, seeded and sliced thin
4 Sprigs Parsley, tied into bundle with white cotton thread
2 1/2 lb Fresh carp, cut into 1-inch slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine onions, vinegar, and 1/4 cup water in a 3-quart Dutch oven. Bring to a boil.
2. Cook, uncovered, over medium heat until all liquid evaporates, stirring often.
3. Add remaining water, vegetable stock, celery, carrot, lemon slices, sugar bundle, and parsley bundle. Bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat to slow-boil and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Add fish. Bring liquid back to a boil and baste the fish several times.
6. Reduce heat to simmering. Cover and cook for 30 minutes.
7. Uncover, raise heat slightly, and slow-cook for an additional 10 minutes.
8. With a slotted spatula, transfer slices to plates. Discard skin.
9. Pull out bones, separating large chunks of flesh to make bones visible. Spoon with some of the hot poaching liquid.
10. Strain contents of pot through a fine sieve into another container. Rinse out the pot and fill with strained broth. Stir in remaining sugar. Bring to a boil over high heat; reduce until about 3/4 cup remains.
11. Pour exuded liquid from fish into the pot.
12. To serve hot, portion individual servings onto warm plates and spoon some of the poaching liquid on top. To serve jelled, arrange fish in a wide casserole in one layer. Pour poaching liquid over it. Cover tightly and chill overnight (poaching liquid will jell). Cut into serving pieces; garnish with watercress.
13. Yield: 4 servings as main course - 6 as appetizer. Serve with beet horseradish. Frances Prince's New Jewish Cuisine
